Closes #

# Types of changes
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)

### Describe Breaking Changes:

# Checklist
- [ ] Linked issue carries an `## 🧭 Implementation Plan` comment.
- [ ] Commits follow Conventional Commits with `(#N)` references.
- [ ] I have added tests to cover my changes.
- [ ] No test was weakened, skipped, deleted, or had its expected values edited to force a pass — the **code** was fixed, not the test. Any test change is an intentional spec change explained in this PR.
- [ ] Feature branch merged `origin/dev` immediately before opening this PR (never rebased).
- [ ] README / docs updated if behavior, commands, or settings changed.
- [ ] Any new user-facing string lives in `CopyStrings.swift` and follows the health-copy rules in `docs/PRD.md` §2.
- [ ] No network code, analytics, or new dependencies introduced.
